results.question: How many patients do we have?
results.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_patients FROM patients

results.question: List all doctors and their specializations
results.sql: SELECT name AS doctor_name, specialization, department FROM doctors ORDER BY name

results.question: Show me appointments for last month
results.sql: SELECT a.id, p.first_name, p.last_name, d.name AS doctor, a.appointment_date, a.status FROM appointments a JOIN patients p ON p.id = a.patient_id JOIN doctors d ON d.id = a.doctor_id WHERE a.appointment_date >= date('now', 'start of month', '-1 month') AND a.appointment_date < date('now', 'start of month') ORDER BY a.appointment_date

results.question: Which doctor has the most appointments?
results.sql: SELECT d.name, COUNT(a.id) AS appointment_count FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY appointment_count DESC LIMIT 1

results.question: What is the total revenue?
results.sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(total_amount), 2) AS total_revenue FROM invoices

results.question: Show revenue by doctor
results.sql: SELECT d.name, ROUND(SUM(i.total_amount), 2) AS total_revenue FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.id = a.doctor_id JOIN invoices i ON a.patient_id = i.patient_id GROUP BY d.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

results.question: How many cancelled appointments last quarter?
results.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cancelled_count FROM appointments WHERE status = 'Cancelled' AND appointment_date >= date('now', '-3 months')

results.question: Top 5 patients by spending
results.sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, ROUND(SUM(i.total_amount), 2) AS total_spending FROM patients p JOIN invoices i ON p.id = i.patient_id GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name ORDER BY total_spending DESC LIMIT 5

results.question: Average treatment cost by specialization
results.sql: SELECT d.specialization, ROUND(AVG(t.cost), 2) AS avg_cost FROM treatments t JOIN appointments a ON a.id = t.appointment_id JOIN doctors d ON d.id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.specialization ORDER BY avg_cost DESC

results.question: Show monthly appointment count for the past 6 months
results.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', appointment_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS total FROM appointments WHERE appointment_date >= date('now', '-6 months') GROUP BY month ORDER BY month
issues: Expected at most 6 rows, got 7 (LIMIT missing or incorrect)

results.question: Which city has the most patients?
results.sql: SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS patient_count FROM patients GROUP BY city ORDER BY patient_count DESC LIMIT 1

results.question: List patients who visited more than 3 times
results.sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, COUNT(a.id) AS visit_count FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON a.patient_id = p.id GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name HAVING COUNT(a.id) > 3 ORDER BY visit_count DESC

results.question: Show unpaid invoices
results.sql: SELECT i.id, p.first_name, p.last_name, i.total_amount, i.paid_amount, i.status, i.invoice_date FROM invoices i JOIN patients p ON p.id = i.patient_id WHERE i.status IN ('Pending', 'Overdue') ORDER BY i.invoice_date DESC

results.question: What percentage of appointments are no-shows?
results.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'No-Show' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS no_show_percentage FROM appointments

results.question: Show the busiest day of the week for appointments
results.sql: SELECT CASE CAST(strftime('%w', appointment_date) AS INTEGER) WHEN 0 THEN 'Sunday' WHEN 1 THEN 'Monday' WHEN 2 THEN 'Tuesday' WHEN 3 THEN 'Wednesday' WHEN 4 THEN 'Thursday' WHEN 5 THEN 'Friday' WHEN 6 THEN 'Saturday' END AS day_name, COUNT(*) AS total FROM appointments GROUP BY strftime('%w', appointment_date) ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 1

results.question: Revenue trend by month
results.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', invoice_date) AS month, ROUND(SUM(total_amount), 2) AS revenue FROM invoices GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

results.question: Average appointment duration by doctor
results.sql: SELECT d.name, ROUND(AVG(t.duration_minutes), 1) AS avg_duration FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.id = a.doctor_id JOIN treatments t ON a.id = t.appointment_id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY avg_duration DESC

results.question: List patients with overdue invoices
results.sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.first_name, p.last_name, p.email, i.total_amount, i.paid_amount, i.invoice_date FROM patients p JOIN invoices i ON p.id = i.patient_id WHERE i.status = 'Overdue' ORDER BY i.invoice_date

results.question: Compare revenue between departments
results.sql: SELECT d.department, ROUND(SUM(i.total_amount), 2) AS total_revenue FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.id = a.doctor_id JOIN invoices i ON a.patient_id = i.patient_id GROUP BY d.department ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

results.question: Show patient registration trend by month
results.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', registered_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS new_patients FROM patients GROUP BY month ORDER BY month
